pattern: Splitting into two equal-sum halves reduces to asking whether some subset reaches exactly half the total, a classic subset-sum reachability solved with a boolean reachability set or 1-D DP.
hints: If the total of all numbers is odd, no split can balance, so the answer is immediately false; otherwise you only need to hit exactly half.
Track which subset sums are reachable as you consider each number, growing the set of achievable totals one element at a time.
Use a boolean array reachable[0..half]; for each number iterate the target downward and mark reachable[t] true when reachable[t - num] was already true, then return reachable[half].

edge_inputs.description: even total but no reachable half
all max-value elements, even count splits
even total odd count where greedy halves fail but DP succeeds

stress.description: build an array with a known equal split by duplicating a random multiset into two halves
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    half = max(1, size // 2)
    base = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(half)]
    nums = base + base[:]
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    return [nums]
stress.note: duplicated multiset guarantees a true partition while staying within length 200

stress.description: random arrays that are usually not partitionable, exercising the false path
